originalTitle: Tesla Cybertruck driver arrested after driving into lake to use 'wade mode', police say
summary: Police said the vehicle was abandoned by the driver after it took on water near the shoreline of Grapevine Lake in north Texas.
content:
A man has been arrested in Texas accused of intentionally driving his Tesla Cybertruck into a lake to use the vehicle's "wade mode" feature.

The Grapevine Police Department said its officers were called to Grapevine Lake in north Texas on Monday to recover the vehicle, which had been abandoned by the driver and passengers after it took on water and became stuck.

Wade mode allows a Cybertruck "to enter and drive through bodies of water, such as rivers or creeks" at a maximum depth of 81.5cm (32in), according to Tesla's online manual.

The driver was arrested on charges of operating a vehicle in a closed section of the lake and for other water safety equipment violations, police said.

The partially submerged vehicle was removed from near the shoreline of the south side of the lake, with the Grapevine Fire Department Water Rescue Team assisting police in the operation.

According to the police statement, "the driver stated he intentionally drove into the lake to use the Cybertruck's 'Wade Mode' feature", after which "the vehicle became disabled and took on water".

Police added that "although a vehicle may be physically capable of entering shallow freshwater areas, doing so can create legal and safety concerns under Texas law".

"We wouldn't encourage willingly driving your vehicle into the water," said Katharina Gamboa of the Grapevine Police Department, in comments reported by the BBC's US news partner CBS News.

"It's a safety concern but it's also a legal concern as well."

The Tesla Cybertruck is an electric pickup truck made of bullet-resistant steel materials and sells for upwards of $70,000 (£52,072). It was launched in 2019 and rolled out to the public in 2023 .

Tesla states in its owner's manual for the vehicle that it is the driver's responsibility "to gauge the depth of any body of water before entering", adding that "damage or water ingress to Cybertruck as a result of driving in water is not covered by the warranty".

Tesla says the maximum wade depth of approximately 32in (81.5cm) is measured from the bottom of the tyre. Where water becomes too deep, the manual advises drivers to return to more shallow water or dry land.

The manufacturer also warns not to drive in deep, fast-flowing bodies of water such as strong currents or rapids.

chineseTitle: 警方称，特斯拉赛博卡车司机因使用“涉水模式”驶入湖中而被捕
chineseSummary: 警方表示，这辆车在德克萨斯州北部格雷普韦恩湖海岸线附近进水后被司机遗弃。
chineseContent:
一名男子在德克萨斯州被捕，被指控故意驾驶特斯拉赛博卡车进入湖中以使用车辆的“涉水模式”功能。

格雷普韦恩警察局表示，其警员周一被派往得克萨斯州北部的格雷普韦恩湖打捞这辆车，这辆车在进水并被困后被司机和乘客遗弃。

根据特斯拉的在线手册，涉水模式允许赛博卡车“进入并驶过最大深度为 81.5 厘米（32 英寸）的水体，例如河流或小溪”。

警方称，这名司机因涉嫌在湖区封闭区域驾驶车辆以及其他违反水上安全设备的行为而被捕。

格雷普韦恩消防局水上救援队协助警方，在湖南岸线附近将部分淹没的车辆打捞出来。

根据警方的声明，“司机表示，他故意开车进入湖中，以使用 Cyber​​truck 的‘涉水模式’功能”，之后“车辆就瘫痪并进水了”。

警方补充说，“尽管车辆可能有能力进入浅淡水区域，但根据德克萨斯州法律，这样做可能会产生法律和安全问题”。

格雷普韦恩警察局的卡塔琳娜·甘博亚 (Katharina Gamboa) 在英国广播公司 (BBC) 美国新闻合作伙伴哥伦比亚广播公司新闻 (CBS News) 报道的评论中表示：“我们不鼓励自愿将车辆开入水中。”

“这是一个安全问题，但也是一个法律问题。”

Tesla Cyber​​truck 是一款由防弹钢材料制成的电动皮卡，售价高达 70,000 美元（52,072 英镑）。它于 2019 年推出，并于 2023 年向公众推出。

特斯拉在其车辆用户手册中指出，驾驶员有责任“在进入任何水体之前测量水体的深度”，并补充说“由于在水中驾驶而导致的 Cyber​​truck 损坏或进水不在保修范围内”。

特斯拉表示，最大涉水深度约为 32 英寸（81.5 厘米），是从轮胎底部测量的。当水变得太深时，手册建议司机返回更浅的水域或干燥的土地。

制造商还警告不要在深水、快速流动的水体中驾驶，例如强水流或急流。

whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
